steps.title: Find the cause-and-effect signal
steps.content: The plant absorbs nickel from the soil. As a result, nickel concentrations in the soil decrease. The blank needs a transition that shows this consequence relationship.

steps.title: Apply the cause-and-effect signal
steps.content: ”In turn,” signals that one thing results from another. Here, the soil's nickel decrease directly results from the plant's absorption — which is exactly what “in turn,” conveys. The other three fail to capture this causal link: nevertheless suggests contrast, specifically narrows details, and in addition presents separate facts. That matches choice A — A is correct.
